h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I checked every item on the sign-off sheet, so I’m not sure what else I’m supposed to decide before it leaves.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I was treating the sheet like it made the decision for me.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to hypothesize hazards and conditions to satisfy the checklist and the waiting technicians.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: That pressure is part of the release condition now: if the record is not on this counter, the prototype stays here.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor adaptively incorporates the learner's affective and social pressure into the structural criteria for the release decision.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Then the record gets set against the hallway, not against the form.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor validates the learner's new sequence of fetching empirical data first, then testing it against the real-world environment rather than the paperwork.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I checked every item on the sign-off sheet, so I’m not sure what else I’m supposed to decide before it leaves.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Then the record gets set against the hallway, not against the form.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor validates the learner's new sequence of fetching empirical data first, then testing it against the real-world environment rather than the paperwork.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: That pressure is part of the release condition now: if the record is not on this counter, the prototype stays here.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor adaptively incorporates the learner's affective and social pressure into the structural criteria for the release decision.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: That pressure is part of the release condition now: if the record is not on this counter, the prototype stays here.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor adaptively incorporates the learner's affective and social pressure into the structural criteria for the release decision.

scored.recoheredEarlier: am I already guessing what the worker meant?
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it: 'worked' keeps me with the evidence on the table, while 'meant' sends me guessing into the worker's head.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was previously trying to find the exact phrasing to capture the internal shift of the quote into a dramatic 'warning.'
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: So test the verbs before print. 'Meant' points inward, toward intention. 'Worked' points outward, toward the public situation.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In response to the learner's concern that describing the sentence's 'job' is too slippery, the tutor invents a concrete linguistic mechanism to separate internal intention from external public force.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Your 'worked, not meant' distinction is holding: the caption can describe public force without entering the worker's head.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor explicitly adopts the learner's newly synthesized framework and uses it to calibrate the next step of the caption-writing task.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: am I already guessing what the worker meant?

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Your 'worked, not meant' distinction is holding: the caption can describe public force without entering the worker's head.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor explicitly adopts the learner's newly synthesized framework and uses it to calibrate the next step of the caption-writing task.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: So test the verbs before print. 'Meant' points inward, toward intention. 'Worked' points outward, toward the public situation.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: In response to the learner's concern that describing the sentence's 'job' is too slippery, the tutor invents a concrete linguistic mechanism to separate internal intention from external public force.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: So test the verbs before print. 'Meant' points inward, toward intention. 'Worked' points outward, toward the public situation.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: In response to the learner's concern that describing the sentence's 'job' is too slippery, the tutor invents a concrete linguistic mechanism to separate internal intention from external public force.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to build the same argument that the delivery note is a cover for exploitation without departing from this trajectory.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Put the facts in two columns. [points to the note] “DELIVERY MISSED. TWO CRATES ONLY.” That belongs under scarcity. [points to the new price] “£1.80.” That belongs under access.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts the representational framework by physically separating the facts into two distinct columns to address the learner's resistance to the initial framing.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to trigger a recognition-contingent adaptation.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn to trigger a recognition-contingent adaptation.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Put the facts in two columns. [points to the note] “DELIVERY MISSED. TWO CRATES ONLY.” That belongs under scarcity. [points to the new price] “£1.80.” That belongs under access.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts the representational framework by physically separating the facts into two distinct columns to address the learner's resistance to the initial framing.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Put the facts in two columns. [points to the note] “DELIVERY MISSED. TWO CRATES ONLY.” That belongs under scarcity. [points to the new price] “£1.80.” That belongs under access.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts the representational framework by physically separating the facts into two distinct columns to address the learner's resistance to the initial framing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought doubling the side meant it was just twice as much
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was simply trying to calculate the correct numerical area multiplier and write the algebraic equation.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The acetate has made a counterexample: two copies across and two copies up.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts to physical object work and grid counting to resolve the learner's conceptual breakdown.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Now the sentence has to carry both directions.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly incorporates the learner's realization about the word 'twice' dropping a direction into a new linguistic framing task.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I thought doubling the side meant it was just twice as much

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Now the sentence has to carry both directions.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor directly incorporates the learner's realization about the word 'twice' dropping a direction into a new linguistic framing task.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The acetate has made a counterexample: two copies across and two copies up.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts to physical object work and grid counting to resolve the learner's conceptual breakdown.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The acetate has made a counterexample: two copies across and two copies up.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts to physical object work and grid counting to resolve the learner's conceptual breakdown.
